description: The figure displays the binary Fe–Be equilibrium phase diagram, with temperature (°C) on the vertical axis and Fe/(Be+Fe) molar ratio (0–1) on the horizontal axis. Key phase fields identified include Liquid, BCC-A2 (ferrite), FCC-A1 (austenite), HCP-A3, and intermetallic line compounds FeBe₁₂, FeBe₅, and FeBe₂, together with associated invariant reactions. The diagram indicates that BCC-A2 accommodates markedly higher Be solubility than FCC-A1, whereas the austenite phase exhibits very limited Be dissolution. As stated in the caption, this map describes the thermodynamic equilibrium relationship between Be and Fe components. Supporting text notes that Be solubility in austenite is less than 1%, forcing excess Be to precipitate as intermetallic compounds or partition to ferrite. This phase-diagram evidence explains the microstructural trend in Fe–30Mn–10Al–1.5C–xBe low-density steels: increasing Be beyond solid-solubility limits leads to ferrite formation around austenite grains and Be-rich compound precipitation within the austenite matrix.
